Politics.headline: Trump set to deliver second term's first State of the Union: What to expect
Politics.detail: President Trump is expected to proclaim the economy booming while endorsing increased military funding and tighter voter ID rules in his State of the Union address. Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger will deliver the Democratic response.

Politics.headline: France restricts US ambassador's access to officials after summons no-show
Politics.detail: France has restricted the US ambassador's access to officials after the ambassador sent an embassy official in his place for a summons from the French Foreign Ministry. This marks the second time the US ambassador has skipped a summons.

Politics.headline: Trump says he's 'ashamed of certain members' of the Supreme Court after it strikes down tariffs
Politics.detail: President Trump has criticized the Supreme Court after it struck down tariffs, stating that he is 'ashamed of certain members'. He also indicated plans to raise tariffs to 15 percent following the ruling.

Politics.headline: Midterm underdogs rally around data center politics
Politics.detail: An increase in data center construction is fueling underdog candidates in key midterm races, as candidates aim to capitalize on concerns about affordability. Democrats from Georgia to Michigan are attempting to leverage concerns about the energy-intensive facilities to their advantage, as voters worry about the impact on electricity bills.

Politics.headline: UK government will release documents about Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's appointment as trade envoy
Politics.detail: The UK government has agreed to release documents pertaining to Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's appointment as trade envoy. Meanwhile, MPs are debating the 'arrogant' Andrew.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's new global tariffs kick in at 10%; Meta strikes $60bn chip deal with AMD
Finance & Economy.detail: Donald Trump's new 10% global tariff comes into effect as Meta agrees to a $60bn deal with chipmaker AMD.

Finance & Economy.headline: India launches 1st round of FTA talks with Israel, inks joint statement for GCC pact
Finance & Economy.detail: India has commenced the first round of Free Trade Agreement talks with Israel and signed a joint statement for a trade pact with the Gulf Cooperation Council.

Finance & Economy.headline: Emerging trends for 2026, and other finance news to know
Finance & Economy.detail: Banks are scaling more autonomous AI systems into core operations, and there's a continuing shift towards private credit as companies seek faster, more flexible funding.

Finance & Economy.headline: Asian shares are mixed after heavy selling of potential AI losers hits Wall Street
Finance & Economy.detail: Shares in Asia are mixed following a slump in U.S. stocks due to heavy selling of companies potentially losing out in the artificial-intelligence boom.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks drop after Trump ramps up his tariffs and investors dump potential AI losers
Finance & Economy.detail: US stocks are declining after President Trump increased tariffs, and investors are selling off companies that could be negatively impacted by AI.

Technology.headline: Meta agrees $60bn deal with chipmaker AMD despite AI bubble fears
Technology.detail: Meta has agreed to a $60 billion deal with chipmaker AMD, demonstrating continued investment in AI despite concerns of an inflated market. The deal aims to bolster Meta's capabilities in AI development and deployment.

Technology.headline: Scientists Create Chip That Generates Brand-New Colors of Light, Cracking a Decades-Old Nonlinear Optics Challenge
Technology.detail: Researchers have created photonic chips that reliably produce new colors of light by harnessing two natural timescales in resonator arrays. This development cracks a decades-old challenge in nonlinear optics.

Technology.headline: AI Rapidly Rendering Cyber Defenses Obsolete: Report
Technology.detail: A new report indicates that the rapid adoption of AI is outpacing enterprise security measures, leaving organizations vulnerable to machine-speed attacks and unmanaged AI risks. This poses a significant challenge for cybersecurity professionals.

Technology.headline: Euro hosting giant hiking prices by up to 50% from April Fool's Day
Technology.detail: A major European hosting provider is increasing prices by up to 50% starting April 1st due to memory shortages. Customers are expressing concern as they face increased costs for hosting services.

Technology.headline: Deep in Congo Basin, vast peatlands quietly store enormous amounts of Earth's carbon -- but new research suggests this ancient vault may be leaking
Technology.detail: Research indicates that peatlands in the Congo Basin, which store large amounts of carbon, may be leaking, which poses concerns about climate change.

Science.headline: Space lasers reveal oceans rising faster than ever
Science.detail: A 30-year analysis shows melting land ice is now the primary driver of rising global sea levels, with oceans rising about 90 millimeters since 1993, mainly due to water mass increase from ice loss in Greenland and mountain glaciers. The rate of sea-level rise is also accelerating.

Science.headline: Massive US study finds higher cancer death rates near nuclear power plants
Science.detail: A nationwide study found that U.S. counties closer to operating nuclear power plants have higher cancer death rates compared to those farther away, even after adjusting for factors like income, education, and lifestyle. The increased cancer mortality was particularly evident among older adults.

Science.headline: How bacteria can reclaim lost energy, nutrients, and clean water from wastewater
Science.detail: A review in Frontiers in Science highlights the potential of microbially-powered technologies to reclaim resources from wastewater, which could power agriculture, global sanitation, and even the wastewater treatment itself. This approach could save money and reduce environmental harm by reclaiming untapped resources from the 359 billion cubic meters of wastewater produced globally each year.

Science.headline: Researchers frustrated with latest MSCA postdoc grant round
Science.detail: The latest funding round for Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions (MSCA) postdoctoral grants has left scientists frustrated after an influx of applications lowered the chance of winning a grant to 9.6%, down from 16.4% the previous year. Many highly rated projects were left unfunded due to the EU's stretched budget, with the European Commission only able to award grants to 1,610 out of 17,000 applicants.

Science.headline: Trashing cancer's 'undruggable' proteins
Science.detail: Scientists have developed protein-like polymers (PLPs) capable of grabbing proteins and directing them to the cell's waste-disposal machinery, where the proteins are degraded and disposed, triggering cancer cell death. The study was published in the journal Nature.

Health.headline: Cardinal Health report highlights cost savings and provider confidence in biosimilars
Health.detail: A new report from Cardinal Health shows biosimilars have generated more than $56 billion in healthcare savings since 2015 and that nearly 99% of providers surveyed feel comfortable explaining biosimilars to patients. The report also anticipates nearly 25 new biosimilar FDA approvals in the next two years in areas like immunology and oncology.

Health.headline: New report underlines the health and economic opportunity from boosting industry clinical trials
Health.detail: A new report from the European Federation of Pharmaceutical Industries and Associations (EFPIA) highlights the significant health, economic and productivity returns that would result from increasing clinical trial activity across Europe. Increasing industry clinical trial activity by 11 percent could deliver an additional £3.5 billion to the European economy, alongside new jobs and clinical trial places.

Health.headline: FDA Proposes New Approval Pathway For Rare Disease Gene Therapy
Health.detail: The Food and Drug Administration is proposing a new system for approving customized drugs and medical treatments for patients with rare or hard-to-treat diseases. The FDA will drop the two-study requirement for new drug approvals, aiming to speed access.

Health.headline: Baby Teeth Expose Lead’s Permanent Toll on Cognition
Health.detail: Research shows that lead exposure can have a permanent impact on cognitive function. The study highlights the importance of reducing lead exposure in children to protect their long-term health.

Health.headline: HANTAVIRUS – ARGENTINA/CHILE: (TANDIL/BIO BIO) MUERTES, ACTIVIDADES DE CONTROL Y PREVENCIÓN
Health.detail: Reports indicate deaths and control/prevention activities related to Hantavirus in Tandil, Argentina and Bio Bio, Chile. This highlights the ongoing concern and efforts to manage the spread of this virus in the region.

Sports.headline: T20 World Cup: Varun Chakaravarthy returns to Chepauk after South Africa debacle
Sports.detail: Varun Chakaravarthy is returning to Chepauk following the T20 World Cup, after a disappointing performance against South Africa. The team is looking to regroup and strategize for upcoming matches.

Sports.headline: 2026 NFL Draft: Would Fernando Mendoza Have Been the Top QB in 2025 Draft Class?
Sports.detail: Analysis of Fernando Mendoza's potential draft position in the 2026 NFL Draft compared to the quarterbacks in the 2025 class is being discussed. Scouts are evaluating his skills and how he measures up against previous prospects.

Sports.headline: Lionesses to play Women's World Cup qualifier at Everton's stadium
Sports.detail: The Lionesses will play a Women's World Cup qualifier at Everton's stadium. This is according to news reports from 2 hours ago.

Sports.headline: T20 World Cup LIVE: Babar Flops Again Despite Good Start, Pakistan 3 Down
Sports.detail: During a T20 World Cup match, Babar Azam failed to capitalize on a promising start, contributing to Pakistan's early setbacks, as the team is now 3 wickets down. Live updates and analysis are ongoing.

Sports.headline: White scores 23 as No. 14 Kansas rebounds with 69-56 win over No. 5 Houston
Sports.detail: Tre White scored 23 points, leading No. 14 Kansas to a 69-56 victory over No. 5 Houston, as Kansas bounced back from a previous defeat. The game took place on Monday night.

Entertainment.headline: Robert Carradine Dies by Suicide: 'Lizzie McGuire' Actor Dead at 71
Entertainment.detail: Robert Carradine, known for playing Sam McGuire on 'Lizzie McGuire,' has died at 71; initial reports indicate suicide as the cause of death. Carradine's role as Lizzie's dad endeared him to fans of the hit Disney Channel series.

Entertainment.headline: BAFTA and BBC apologize for racial slur during awards show
Entertainment.detail: The British Academy Film Awards and BBC have issued an apology after a racial slur was broadcast during Sunday's show while two stars of the film “Sinners” were onstage. The offensive outburst came from an audience member with Tourette's syndrome.

Entertainment.headline: Nick Reiner Pleads Not Guilty to Murder
Entertainment.detail: Nick Reiner, son of Rob Reiner, has pleaded not guilty in Los Angeles to the killing of his parents, Rob Reiner and Michele Singer Reiner. He was arrested and charged with two counts of first-degree murder following the deaths of his parents.

Entertainment.headline: Oprah Winfrey delivers Tayari Jones' 'Kin' news face-to-face
Entertainment.detail: Oprah Winfrey surprised author Tayari Jones at the Penguin Random House offices in midtown Manhattan to announce that Jones' novel “Kin” is her new book club pick. This marks the second time Winfrey has selected a novel by Jones, who previously wrote “An American Marriage”.

Entertainment.headline: The Kerala Story 2 faces 16 CBFC cuts; rape and kissing scenes trimmed
Entertainment.detail: The Censor Board has ordered approximately 16 cuts or alterations to the film The Kerala Story 2, reducing a rape scene by 20 seconds and a lip-lock scene by 7 seconds. Kissing scenes and depictions of sexual violence in the film have been reduced by 50%.

Crime & Justice.headline: Court backlog will take decade to fall to pre-Covid levels despite overhaul, says MoJ
Crime & Justice.detail: The backlog in criminal courts in England and Wales is projected to take a decade to return to pre-COVID levels, even with radical changes like curtailing jury trials, according to the Ministry of Justice. Justice Secretary David Lammy insists on pushing forward with jury trial changes to prevent the backlog from rising exponentially.

Crime & Justice.headline: Aggressive immigration enforcement opens way for federal criminal defendants to avoid justice
Crime & Justice.detail: Federal judges are dismissing criminal indictments against defendants held in immigration detention due to access issues, as aggressive deportation efforts create a collision between immigration priorities and criminal prosecutions. A defendant in federal custody for a large jewelry heist was deported to Ecuador, effectively ending the case against him, sparking outrage from victims.

Crime & Justice.headline: Brazil: Marielle Franco and Mãe Bernadette murder trials will test state's resolve to deliver justice for human rights defenders
Crime & Justice.detail: Amnesty International states that Brazil faces a crucial test of its commitment to addressing impunity for attacks on human rights defenders as trials begin for the murders of Marielle Franco, Anderson Gomes, and Maria Bernadete Pacífico. The trials, taking place in the Federal Supreme Court and the Bahia Jury Court, are long overdue and highlight the Brazilian justice system's failures in providing effective responses to victims and their families.

Crime & Justice.headline: Violent crime drops in SF as major U.S. cities see broad declines
Crime & Justice.detail: Violent crime has sharply decreased across major American cities, including San Francisco, in 2025, signaling a potential recovery from the pandemic era. San Francisco saw decreases in reports of aggravated assault, robbery, rape, and homicide.

Crime & Justice.headline: Opening arguments to begin in Prairieland ICE detention center shooting
Crime & Justice.detail: Opening arguments are set to begin in the Prairieland ICE detention center shooting case, with multiple defendants facing charges including rioting, providing material support to terrorists, and attempted murder of officers. The case involves allegations of concealing evidence and vandalizing property, with potential sentences ranging from 10 years to life in prison if convicted.

Education.headline: Grants Pass expands free early learning program amid rising demand
Education.detail: The Grants Pass School District is set to open a second early learning site next year to double preschool capacity due to increasing demand for affordable early childhood education, as the current site's enrollment and waitlist continue to grow. The program, which launched in 2023, offers preschool, playgroups, and workshops and aims to support kids entering kindergarten by addressing skill gaps.

Education.headline: Springfield school board approves new acting superintendent
Education.detail: Jodi O'Mara, former superintendent of Mapleton School District, has been unanimously approved by the Springfield Board of Education to serve as acting superintendent of Springfield Public Schools for the next four months, starting March 1. The school board will also be working to choose a new member to fill its fifth seat.

Education.headline: Opinion: Connecticut's education crisis
Education.detail: Connecticut's public schools are facing a crisis with 98% of teachers reporting stress and burnout as a top concern, leading nearly three-quarters to consider early retirement, and the state lists 11 critical shortage areas. Systemic changes are needed, including increased base pay, reduced non-teaching duties, more planning time, and greater mental health support to make teaching a viable, respected, long-term career.

Education.headline: WorldQuant University and Renewed Hope to expand free online education in Nigeria
Education.detail: WorldQuant University is partnering with Renewed Hope Global to broaden access to its free online academic programs for Nigerian youth, linking the initiative with a presidency-endorsed platform aligned with Nigeria's Renewed Hope Administration. The collaboration aims to integrate online technical education into the national reform narrative, emphasizing skills development and digital capacity as economic priorities, with WorldQuant University currently supporting over 5,000 learners in Nigeria.

Education.headline: The full potential of STEM education: How the ExxonMobil Foundation is making STEM more accessible
Education.detail: The ExxonMobil Foundation is working to make STEM more accessible, noting that STEM jobs are projected to grow more than 10% over the next decade, but many positions risk going unfilled due to a shortage of skilled candidates. Since 2024, the Foundation has piloted three STEM initiatives aimed at building a stronger and more inclusive STEM talent pipeline.

Lifestyle.headline: Met Monday: Decoding the most anticipated dress code of 2026
Lifestyle.detail: An article decoding the potential fashion themes and expectations for the upcoming Met Gala in 2026.

Lifestyle.headline: I worked for 38 years believing retirement would feel like freedom, and instead it felt like I'd been fired from the only identity anyone ever cared about
Lifestyle.detail: A personal essay reflecting on the challenges of transitioning from a long career as a teacher to retirement and the unexpected feelings of loss and lack of purpose.

Lifestyle.headline: Are Media reports print audience growth across lifestyle portfolio
Lifestyle.detail: Are Media, an Australian women's lifestyle network, reported growth in print readership across its homes and lifestyle publications, with Better Homes & Gardens and Australian Gourmet Traveller showing notable increases. The company reaches over 7.3 million Australians monthly across various platforms.

Lifestyle.headline: Mixue, the world's biggest fast-food chain, has landed in the U.S. What is it and how does it taste?
Lifestyle.detail: Mixue, known for its affordable vanilla cones and fruity drinks, has expanded to the United States after becoming the largest fast-food chain globally. The chain is ubiquitous in China for its cheap offerings.

Lifestyle.headline: Heavy snow is recipe for serious heart attack risk, experts say: What to know
Lifestyle.detail: Doctors warn that shoveling heavy, wet snow and exposure to frigid winds can strain the heart and raise injury risk, especially for inactive adults. This highlights potential health risks associated with winter weather and physical exertion.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Resist attempts to block ICE
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Sylvania Resource Conservation and Equal Access Ordinance would prohibit city employees from using city resources to investigate citizenship status, except when required by law, or for the provision of a specific city service, or when necessary for a criminal investigation. Toledo City Council is also facing pressure from advocates of Palestinians in Gaza to make national foreign policy, which the council is encouraged to decline.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A deal that settles nothing
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Indonesia's finalized bilateral trade agreement with the United States faces uncertainty after the US Supreme Court's ruling on President Trump's tariff policy. There are concerns about the lack of reciprocity in the deal, which imposes a 19 percent tariff on most Indonesian goods while granting US firms free access.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: A bad bet for the Colorado Lottery
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A bipartisan group of lawmakers introduced Senate Bill 26-117 to reverse the lottery commission's new policies, keeping lottery sales in person and in cash (or debit card) to protect against problem gambling. The lawmakers' request to have input on the policy changes was disregarded by the commission, raising concerns about the potential costs of the policy decision.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Marco Rubio's silk-stocking diplomacy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio delivered a message similar to J.D. Vance's from last year, but swaddled in diplomatic gauze, addressing the Munich Security Conference. Rubio's promise of a future is not a vision of something to be built, but the past projected forward, nostalgia packaged as a goal.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Shaping the next generation of leaders for our country's progress
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Opinion piece discussing the shaping of the next generation of leaders.

World.headline: Russia accuses UK and France of planning to give Ukraine nuclear bomb
World.detail: Russia alleges that the UK and France are considering providing Ukraine with nuclear weapons, escalating tensions surrounding the ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Zelenskyy urges Trump to visit Ukraine in speech marking invasion anniversary
World.detail: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy invited Donald Trump to visit Ukraine in a speech commemorating the anniversary of the Russian invasion.

World.headline: Mexican security forces kill drug cartel boss 'El Mencho'
World.detail: Mexican forces killed Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, also known as “El Mencho,” the head of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, leading to widespread violence and travel alerts for Americans in parts of Mexico.

World.headline: Taiwan rattled by 5.6 magnitude quake, no reports of damage
World.detail: A 5.6 magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of Taiwan's northeastern county of Yilan, shaking buildings in Taipei, but no damage was reported.

World.headline: New U.S. tariffs take effect today after Supreme Court ruling
World.detail: New tariffs imposed by the U.S. went into effect today following a Supreme Court ruling.

Zimbabwe.headline: Citizens push back on Mnangagwa's term extension
Zimbabwe.detail: Citizens are reportedly resisting efforts to extend President Mnangagwa's term, raising concerns about democratic principles.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim, Malawi archivists ink co-operation deal
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe and Malawi archivists have signed a cooperation deal to enhance collaboration in preserving historical records.

Zimbabwe.headline: SA fast food chain makes Zim bow
Zimbabwe.detail: A South African fast-food chain is expanding its presence by opening a branch in Zimbabwe.

Zimbabwe.headline: AMH appoints Takura Mapfumo general manager
Zimbabwe.detail: AMH (Alpha Media Holdings) has appointed Takura Mapfumo as its new General Manager.

Zimbabwe.headline: Benza fights drug abuse in his constituency through sport
Zimbabwe.detail: Benza is using sports initiatives to combat drug abuse in his constituency.
